questions.question: Who is a consumer?
questions.answer: A consumer is a person who buys goods or services for personal use.

questions.question: What is consumer exploitation?
questions.answer: Consumer exploitation is unfair treatment of consumers by sellers.

questions.question: Name one example of unfair trade practice.
questions.answer: Selling goods above the MRP is an unfair trade practice.

questions.question: What is adulteration?
questions.answer: Adulteration is mixing inferior substances in goods to reduce quality.

questions.question: What is the Right to Safety?
questions.answer: The Right to Safety protects consumers against hazardous goods and services.

questions.question: What is the Right to Information?
questions.answer: The Right to Information ensures consumers receive complete product details.

questions.question: What is the Right to Choose?
questions.answer: The Right to Choose ensures consumers can select from a variety of goods at fair prices.

questions.question: What is the Right to Seek Redressal?
questions.answer: It is the right to get compensation for defective goods or unfair practices.

questions.question: What does the Consumer Protection Act provide?
questions.answer: It provides legal protection and a mechanism for resolving consumer disputes.

questions.question: In which year was the Consumer Protection Act first passed?
questions.answer: The Consumer Protection Act was first passed in 1986.

questions.question: Name the three levels of consumer courts.
questions.answer: District Forum, State Commission and National Commission.

questions.question: Why is a bill important for consumers?
questions.answer: A bill acts as proof of purchase for filing complaints.

questions.question: What is a misleading advertisement?
questions.answer: It is an advertisement that gives false information about a product.

questions.question: What does the ISI mark indicate?
questions.answer: ISI mark indicates standard quality certification.

questions.question: What does Agmark certify?
questions.answer: Agmark certifies the quality of agricultural products.

questions.question: What does Hallmark certify?
questions.answer: Hallmark certifies the purity of gold jewellery.

questions.question: Why is consumer awareness important?
questions.answer: It helps consumers protect themselves from exploitation.

questions.question: What is hoarding?
questions.answer: Hoarding is storing goods to create artificial shortage and increase prices.

questions.question: What is black marketing?
questions.answer: Black marketing is selling goods illegally at high prices.

questions.question: Who can file a complaint in consumer court?
questions.answer: Any consumer who faces unfair trade practice can file a complaint.

questions.question: What is the role of NGOs in consumer movement?
questions.answer: NGOs spread awareness and support consumers in filing complaints.

questions.question: What is the Right to Consumer Education?
questions.answer: It ensures consumers are informed about their rights and responsibilities.

questions.question: Why are standardisation marks important?
questions.answer: They assure consumers about product quality and safety.

questions.question: What is meant by compensation in consumer court?
questions.answer: Compensation is the amount awarded to consumers for loss or damage.

questions.question: What is one responsibility of a consumer?
questions.answer: A consumer should check product details and demand a proper bill.
